name: Pescespada Alle Brace (Wood Fire Grilled Swordfish)
recipeIngredient: 3 Fennel Bulbs, Cleaned & Trimmed, cut into quarters
2 Large Garlic Cloves, peeled and chopped fine
½ Cup Italian Parsley, chopped fine
2 Tablespoons Fresh Oregano or 1 Teaspoon Dried Oregano
¼ Teaspoon Fresh Ground Black Pepper
recipeInstructions:
Cut into 6-ounce slices, about ¾-inch thick.
1. Place the fennel quarters in a casserole dish. Pour over the chicken stock and sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste. Cover and bake in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven for about 1 hour, basting often. The fennel should be tender but not mushy. Drain and set aside. Use the pan liquid for soups or other dishes.
2. Boil the Green Beans in lightly salted water for about 4 to 5 minutes. Drain and set aside.
3. Rub olive oil on the swordfish slices. Grill them for about 4 minutes on each side, adding salt and pepper to taste. If you don’t have a wood-fired grill, broil or barbecue the fish instead.
4. To make the sauce, combine garlic, parsley, oregano, lemon juice, and hot water in a bowl. Mix well with a spoon. Add olive oil, salt, and black pepper, then mix again. It's best to prepare this sauce several hours ahead; mix it energetically before using.
5. Heat the cooked fennel in a 375°F (190°C) oven for about 20 minutes.
6. Heat the green beans in a skillet with a few drops of olive oil and lemon juice, seasoning with salt and pepper.
7. When the fish is done, place it on a heated plate and garnish with vegetables. Spoon sauce over the fish or serve at the table.
Serves 6.
